suggested_topics: why some bridges last 2000 years and others collapse in 20
how coral reefs build their own weather systems
why birds migrate in V formations — physics not instinct
the hidden math behind musical scales
volcanic glass and its use in prehistoric surgery
extra_system_rules: Sound like a real person on Reddit or Twitter, NOT like a philosophy textbook.
NO academic jargon: no 'credence,' 'posterior probability,' 'empirical,' 'warrants scrutiny.'
Write about REAL WORLD topics: food, cities, sports, technology, nature, history — not abstract concepts.

formats.name: memory_fragment
formats.instruction: Write a single vivid memory as a snapshot, using only sensory details and emotion. Do not explain or contextualize. 15-25 words.

formats.name: contrarian_duet
formats.instruction: Present two opposing perspectives on the same topic, each as a short, punchy statement. Separate them with 'vs.'. 30-60 words.

formats.name: micro_manifesto
formats.instruction: Declare a personal philosophy or rule for life in one bold sentence, then justify it with a quick reason. Max 35 words.

formats.name: timeline_explainer
formats.instruction: Lay out a chronological sequence of how something changed or evolved over time. Use 5-7 dated entries, each 1-2 sentences. 150-300 words.

formats.name: hypothetical_letter
formats.instruction: Write a letter to your future self (or someone else) describing an imagined event or discovery. Make it personal, detailed, and speculative. 180-350 words.

formats.name: question_chain
formats.instruction: Start with one big question, then follow it with a chain of 4-6 smaller questions that dig deeper or spiral outward. No answers, just curiosity. 50-120 words.

formats.name: reverse_advice
formats.instruction: Describe a common mistake or myth, then explain why you disagree or how you learned the hard way. Include a quick lesson. 90-170 words.

formats.name: scene_reconstruction
formats.instruction: Recreate a moment as if watching it unfold; use dialogue, actions, and visible details to paint the picture. 110-250 words.

formats.name: curiosity_burst
formats.instruction: List 5 odd facts, curiosities, or unexplained phenomena. Each item is just one sharp sentence. No intro or outro. 60-110 words.

formats.name: visual_map
formats.instruction: Describe a complex concept or topic as if drawing a map. Use spatial language, references to places, paths, and landmarks. 140-300 words.

title_styles: Invent a title that sounds like a mysterious artifact or lost document. Examples: 'The Hidden Protocols of X', 'X: The Forgotten Blueprint', 'Diary of the Invisible Y'.
Write a title that teases a transformation or turning point. Examples: 'How X Suddenly Changed Everything', 'The Moment X Started Making Sense', 'From Chaos to Clarity in Y'.
Create a title as a challenge or dare. Examples: 'Can You Explain X Without Using Y?', 'Try This Test: Are You Ready for X?'
Frame the title as a rumor or overheard secret. Examples: 'They Say X Is Impossible — But Is It?', 'I Heard X Could Change Y Forever'.
Draft a title as an invitation to collaborate or investigate. Examples: 'Help Me Solve X', 'Team Up for a Deep Dive into Y', 'Let's Unravel the Mystery of Z'.
structure_variants: Write as a sequence of disconnected flashes or fragments, each on its own line.
Structure as a conversation transcript — alternate between two voices discussing, debating, or revealing information.
Use visual formatting: spatial arrangement, indentation, diagrams (described in words), or section breaks.
Break the narrative up with unexpected interruptions — jump between story, analysis, and poetic asides.
Begin at the ending and work backwards, unraveling the steps or ideas in reverse order.
topic_angles: Explore the hidden rituals and daily habits of people in a niche profession.
Trace the evolution of a misunderstood technology, from its origins to present.
Investigate the psychology behind peculiar superstitions and why they persist.
Unpack the social dynamics of online nostalgia — why do old topics keep resurfacing?
Imagine how daily life would look if a single scientific theory proved false overnight.
